element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic enemy pattern recognition; teaches jumping and attacking stationary/slow-moving targets
Core combat mechanic introduced at level start
First power-up type; teaches pickup collection

element_introduction_order.element: Falling/projectile instruments (falling guitars, shooting bagpipes, clarinets with missiles)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduces vertical hazard avoidance and timing
Expands combat capability; allows screen coverage increase

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental hazards (steam blasts, grease, falling objects)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Teaches obstacle avoidance separate from enemy combat
Emergency relief mechanic for crowd control
Automatic targeting variant of guitar attack
Final challenge requiring synthesis of all learned mechanics
Persistent resource management; thumbs-up icons increase worthiness
Risk/reward for exploration and skilled play

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting worthiness items between enemy waves
Screen-clear moments with sparse enemy presence
typical_tension_triggers: Environmental hazards combined with enemy attacks
Swarm enemies (kazoos in swarms, Beatles creatures)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating surrealist chaos with crowd-control mechanics; early levels introduce individual threats, mid-levels combine hazards with enemies, final level concentrates all mechanics into boss confrontation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Kramer's Music Store (Level 1) teaches basic combat with stationary and slow-moving sentient instruments; low complexity enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stan Mikita's Donut Shop and Gasworks (Levels 2-3) introduce environmental hazards, swarm mechanics, projectile variety, and power-up synergy; density increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fantasy Suburb (Level 4) concentrates all mechanics into Zantar boss showdown; requires synthesis of movement, timing, and combat mastery
notable_spikes: Falling guitars and projectile instruments in Level 1 require vertical spacing awareness
Kazoo swarms and disco ball projectiles in Level 3 demand crowd management
Zantar boss encounter tests all accumulated skills

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level's enemies are contextually appropriate sentient objects matching the location theme (instruments at music store, food items at donut shop, club objects at Gasworks)
Early levels feature stationary or slow-moving threats; mid-levels introduce off-screen projectiles and swarms; maintains pressure without overwhelming
Progression from basic amp enhancement → multi-fire chorus → trajectory variation (distortion) → emergency mechanics (Schwing, homing); reinforces mastery depth
Level 2 places Wayne in shrunken state at donut shop; smaller scale intensifies relative hazard perception and enemy density impact
